meta.description: Verses. Seals. The sacred number. The rarest form. The full name ADZMYST is a heptad. Most heptads are variations on the sacred sequence. A heptad is never casual — it is a complete utterance, a seal, a verse of scripture. Beyond seven glyphs, you are not speaking. You are composing.

heptads.name: ADZMYST
heptads.gloss: strength → door → cut → chaos → hand → pillar → completion
heptads.interpretation: The full sacred name. The breath itself. Power opens, divides, enters the deep, is shaped, stands, and is sealed. The complete arc of creation from origin to signature.
heptads.usage: The divine heptad. Spoken only in completion. Carved only on finished works. The name that contains all other names. To speak it is to seal.
heptads.note: This is the canonical heptad. All others are variations. ADZMYST is the measure against which all heptads are judged.

heptads.name: TSY MZDA
heptads.gloss: completion → pillar → hand → chaos → cut → door → strength
heptads.interpretation: The full inversive. The seal stands, acts upon chaos, divides it, opens a passage, and the passage empowers. The name spoken backward — the breath returning to its source.
heptads.usage: Spoken at endings that are also beginnings. The unsealing. The return. 'What was finished now begins again.'

heptads.name: AMY DZST
heptads.gloss: strength → chaos → hand → door → cut → pillar → completion
heptads.interpretation: Power enters the deep, is shaped, opens a passage, divides, stands, and is sealed. The variation where chaos is entered before the door is found.
heptads.usage: Spoken by those who entered the unknown before finding the way. The pioneer's heptad.

heptads.name: MM DAYST
heptads.gloss: chaos, deepens → door → strength → hand → pillar → completion
heptads.interpretation: The deep fully acknowledged, then the door, then power, then the hand, then the pillar, then the seal. The heptad of emergence from darkness.
heptads.usage: Spoken by those who have survived the deep. The survivor's seal. 'I knew the deep. Then everything else followed.'

heptads.name: SS DMYAT
heptads.gloss: pillar, upheld → door → chaos → hand → strength → completion
heptads.interpretation: The pillar fully affirmed, then a door into chaos, then the hand, then power, then the seal. The heptad of stability that opens into the unknown.
heptads.usage: Spoken by those who leave safety to create. 'I stood. I held. Then I opened the door.'

heptads.name: ADZ MMYT
heptads.gloss: strength → door → cut → chaos, deepens → hand → completion
heptads.interpretation: Power opens, divides, and enters the deep — the deep, the deep — then the hand shapes and seals. The heptad where chaos is dwelt in before action.
heptads.usage: Spoken by those who must fully know the deep before they can shape it. The diver's heptad.

heptads.name: YZ MADST
heptads.gloss: hand → cut → chaos → strength → door → pillar → completion
heptads.interpretation: The hand takes the blade, cuts the deep, draws power, opens a passage, the passage stands, and is sealed. The heptad of the pioneer who builds.
heptads.usage: Spoken at foundings. The settler's seal. 'I cut. I took. I opened. I built. It is done.'

heptads.name: TT ADMYS
heptads.gloss: completion, sealed → strength → door → chaos → hand → pillar
heptads.interpretation: It is finished, it is finished — and from this, strength opens a door into chaos, chaos is shaped, and the shaping stands. The double seal that begins a new arc.
heptads.usage: Spoken at the end of one age and the beginning of another. The heptad of transition.

heptads.name: AT MYSDZ
heptads.gloss: strength → completion → chaos → hand → pillar → door → cut
heptads.interpretation: Power, sealed, enters the deep, is shaped, stands, opens, and divides. The heptad of the finished work that continues to act and separate.
heptads.usage: Spoken at the tombs of founders whose works outlive them. The elegiac heptad.

heptads.name: TTT ADZM
heptads.gloss: completion, sealed, sealed → strength → door → cut → chaos
heptads.interpretation: It is finished, it is finished, it is finished — and from the triple seal, strength opens, divides, and enters the deep. The final seal that becomes a new beginning. The end of the Book of Breath.
heptads.usage: Spoken only once. At the end of all things. The seal of seals opening outward. 'It is done. It is done. It is done. And now: the deep.'
